APPLY FOR WIC ONLINE
WIC is an equal opportunity provider.
WIC provides a friendly, supportive environment to learn about nutrition for families, breastfeeding support, and access to healthy foods.
WIC. Here to Help.
WIC is here to help you with:
Information about Health and Nutrition
Breastfeeding support and resources
Monthly benefits for healthy and fresh foods
Referrals to medical providers and community services
Call WIC today to see if you qualify!
​
Kings County WIC Program
(559) 582-0180
You may qualify if you:
Are pregnant, breastfeeding, or just had a baby;
Have a child under age 5;
Have a low to medium income; and/or
Receive Medi-Cal, TANF, or CalFresh benefits; and
Live in California
You may qualify if your family's income meets WIC guidelines.
To find out more, visit
myfamily.wic.ca.gov/
Newly pregnant women, migrant workers, and working families are encouraged to apply.

Nutriton Services
Breastfeeding
Here at WIC, we know that breastfeeding is very important to a baby's nutrition, and that's why we are here to help you every set of the way. Our lactation specialists are available to help you and your baby in your breastfeeding journey.
Breastfeeding provides a variety of benefits for both baby and mom. Not only does it provide all the nutrition your baby needs, but it also strengthens the bond between mom and baby
Here are some of the other reasons why breastfeeding is a great choice:
Helps mom get back to pre-pregnancy weight
Gives your baby all the antibodies they need to fight infections
Reduce the chance of obesity for baby
Reduces stress between both mom and baby
Baby is less likely to develop asthma and allergies
WIC also has many handouts for Breastfeeding assistance and support groups.
Guides to Breastfeeding
How to Express Breastmilk by hand
Laws that protect your right to breastfeed
Returning to work resources
And much more!
For questions please call the Hanford WIC office at: (559) 582-0180

Breastfeeding
WIC Card Information
The WIC card is available to every WIC participant.
​
The WIC card is like a debit card that holds all of your WIC benefits each month. As soon an your benefits are issued for the month, your card is automatically loaded up and ready for use.
You can use your WIC card to buy all of your WIC approved foods at WIC eligible stores.
Don't forget about the WIC App!
With the Mobile WIC App, you can manage things like:
Checking your WIC balance
Scanning foods to see if they are WIC approved
Getting information on your nearest WIC clinics and approved WIC stores
Finding other resources that may benefit you
And much more!
Be sure to check out these WIC card videos for more information
English
Español
To schedule your first appointment with WIC to receive your own WIC card, call us at our main Kings County office at (559) 582-0180
